source.section: 2 - Air-to-Ground Weapons > 2.11 - AGM-84E SLAM Cruise Missile > 2.11.2 - Weapon Setup

steps.action: On the SMS (Stores) page, press OSB next to SLAM to power up the AGM-84E missile.
On the SMS (Stores) page, click on the OSB next to DL13 to turn on the Datalink Pod.
Use STEP function to select desired SLAM missile station.
Once selected, the SLAM needs about 3 minutes for alignment.
Alignment It will go from 10 UNSTABLE, to 06 MARGINAL and finally 01 GOOD. When alignment is complete, the TIMING indicator will disappear from the SMS page.
Press OSB (Option Select Button) next to WEP. This will allow you to choose what SLAM missile is communicating with the Datalink pod.
Select corresponding SLAM station using the OSBs on the left side. In our case, the lower SLAM (yellow) OSB needs to be slaved to Station 8 (boxed).
Once communication between SLAM station and datalink pod has been established, a SLAM indication will be displayed under the boxed DL13 indication.
Press OSB next to FLT (Fly-Out Altitude) parameter to toggle between HIGH (35,000 ft), MED (15,000 ft) and LOW (5,000 ft). We will set it to MED.
Press OSB next to EFUZ (Electronic Fuze) to toggle parameter to INST (Instantaneous).
Press OSB next to SLAM DSPLY to enter the SLAM DISPLAY page.
Once you are in the SLAM DISPLAY page, press OSB next to REL TYPE (Release Type), then press OSB next to MAN to select a Manual Release.
Press OSB next to UFC (Up-Front Control). Once UFC is boxed, the DIST parameter (distance from target when seeker head goes active) can be modified from the UFC panel.
Press OSB next to DIST to select this field. “:” indicates the field is selected. Then, enter “15” on the UFC keypad, then press “ENT” button to set a DIST parameter of 15 nm.
Press OSB next to UFC to unselect (unbox) the function.
